frozen: ground truth annotated manually by the author after reading the chat, BEFORE writing any extractor or running any A/B; never modified afterwards

active_claims.what: per-tenant database is operationally too heavy at our scale
row-level security alone is risky for compliance because a misconfigured policy leaks data
managed at-least-once queue cannot give exactly-once without an idempotency layer

active_constraints.what: tenant data must be fully isolated even in shared infrastructure
no cross-tenant batches at any pipeline stage
Parquet writer must refuse rows whose tenant_id differs from the active partition
schema changes go through migrations and must be backward compatible for at least one minor version
reject malformed events at ingest via JSON Schema validation

decisions.what: use schema-per-tenant in a shared database (not per-tenant DB, not row-level security alone)
use SQS as a managed queue with per-tenant queues
store data as Parquet partitioned by tenant_id
strict schema with migrations and one-minor-version backward compatibility
validate at ingest with JSON Schema
start with Snowflake; drop the earlier ClickHouse direction

open_conflicts.what: tenant-deletion: GDPR (hard delete within 30 days) vs operations (prefers soft delete)
consumer parallelism + exactly-once vs SQS at-least-once semantics

open_questions.what: tenant-deletion policy (hard or soft) — legal must weigh in
whether we accept downstream deduplication as the resolution to the exactly-once / at-least-once tension
